title: Data Engineer
description:
*At* 
***Norrin***
*, we’re shaping the era of Enterprise Intelligence. Our vision is to transform business productivity through data & AI by creating solutions that deliver measurable impact. As a Microsoft technology partner, we are at the forefront of reshaping how businesses operate across the Nordics. What defines us is deep technical expertise, a creative engineering mindset, and a people-first culture. You’ll have the chance to grow in a direction that matters to you while building solutions that make a real difference. With a strong presence in Finland and growing operations in Sweden, the United States, Thailand and Vietnam, we continue to expand our Nordic roots internationally. Most recently, Swedish company* 
***BizOne***
*joined the Norrin Group, further strengthening our presence across the Nordics.*
 We’re looking for
 **Data Engineers** 
 to join our growing team in Stockholm. You might be a data enthusiast with a couple of years under your belt or already a more seasoned professional. What matters most to us is your willingness to keep learning, take ownership, and work on solutions that create clear business value.
   

  

 In this role, you will get to work on a variety of cloud-based customer projects, designing and building modern data platforms and analytics solutions using Azure Data Platform services, Databricks, and AI-enabled tools. Your work may include data modelling, integrations, and architectural design, always in close collaboration with customers and internal experts. You will also be part of Norrin’s Data Engineering Guild, a community where knowledge is shared, skills are developed together, and people actively support each other’s growth.
   

  

**🌱Why Norrin?**
* People-first culture: A workplace where you can be yourself. Our culture is built on openness, equality, and mutual respect
* Flexible working model: Our hybrid model offers the best of both worlds, flexibility in your daily life and in-person collaboration when it moves things forward
* Fair compensation: A competitive salary that reflects your skills and experience
* Grow with us: We encourage continuous learning and development in a direction that matters to you


**✨ We value**
* 2-5 years of experience as a Data Engineer or in analytics solution development
* Experience building Databricks and/or Fabric-based solutions in an Azure environment
* Understanding of data warehousing, data modelling, and integrations
* Good collaboration skills and a customer-oriented mindset
* Curiosity and initiative to keep learning new things and stay up to date
* Ability to communicate technical solutions clearly, also to non-technical stakeholders
* Fluent Swedish and English skills


**🛠️ Your technical toolkit**
* Strong skills in SQL and Python/PySpark
* Experience building data pipelines using Databricks, dbt, Microsoft Fabric or similar
* Solid understanding of data security and privacy principles
* Experience with dimensional modelling
* Knowledge of DevOps and CI/CD principles


**Experience With**
 ➕
 **What’s a plus (but not required)**
* Power BI and automated reporting
* GenAI or machine learning, including MLOps
* Cloud data warehouses such as Snowflake.


**📩 Could this be your next step?**
 We’re excited to hear from you. Please submit your application as soon as possible. We will move forward with the recruitment process as soon as we find the right candidate.

title: Manager - Stockholm | BCG Platinion
description:
Who We Are
   

  

 Do you want to turn the challenges of digitalization into the solutions of the future? Join BCG Platinion in tackling the core IT issues of leading global companies. Deploy customized platforms, architectures, strategies, and organizations to leverage the digital potential of our customers and drive true innovation, with state-of-the-art tools such as cloud computing, AI, blockchain, microservices, and containerization. Add your individual strengths to our unique team, always there to support you. Together with Boston Consulting Group and our clients, we turn digital visions into reality.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

 As a Manager at BCG Platinion in the Nordics, you are responsible for the execution and management of our national and international client projects. We collaborate with colleagues from BCG Platinion and/or the Boston Consulting Group on site and directly with the client company. The Manager role incorporates structuring the project and performing the required resource planning. Besides the functional navigation of the project members, you will also be involved operationally in the project business. Together with our clients, you will develop convincing IT strategies, concepts and solutions, applying technical know-how and extensive experience in structuring IT projects, just as analytical and conceptual skills. We independently support our clients, present our results to their decision makers, and agree on the next steps.
   

  

 What You'll Bring
   

  

**You can help us make a difference if you**
* bring a completed a degree in computer science, (business) informatics, or a related area with above-average grades
* have at least eight years of substantial experience in IT consulting and the lead design and management of IT strategy and implementation projects
* already captured project-related management experience as the lead of teams with more than five people
* know how to plan IT initiatives and build solid business cases
* optimally steer decisions towards the goal and master escalating situations with an eye to solutions - even in complex organizational structures
* have successfully acquired consulting projects
* are excellent at understanding and communicating complex topics in English


 Who You'll Work With
   

  

**Shape the world of tomorrow**
* Independently contribute to build close client relationships - and as their trusted advisor, you are the first to whom they turn

title: Managing Partner - Defence
description:
**Managing Partner**
**IT Strategy Consulting – Defence sector
   

  

 Sweden (Hybrid)**
 Backed by the incredible insight of Gartner Research, Gartner Consulting helps the world’s leading organisations to stay ahead of the digital curve.
   

  

**What we do:**
 Gartner’s Consulting business provides independent support for International organisations across the Defence industry and National Governments. Providing 'right practice' data driven support to decision making, informed by world class research across a range of current and emerging digital challenges, our teams aim to save taxpayers money and improve citizen outcomes for national, regional and local government.
   

  

 We are currently looking for highly talented Defence professional, ideally with consulting experience (at Senior Manager, Associate Director, Director, Managing Director, Engagement Manager, Associate Partner, Client Director, or Principal level), with a strong understanding of technology and strategic transformation, to join our world-class Strategy Consulting team helping Defence and Public Sector clients.
   

  

**What you will do:**
 The Managing Partner, Defense will provide technology foresight, market intelligence to senior leaders within Defense and military organisations. Strategic Planning for the CEO, CDO, CFO, CIO and senior IT executives ranging from roadmaps and operating models, to organisational design and governance, enabling data driven decision making, innovation and IT cost optimisation
   

  

**What you need:**
 We seek candidates with the ability to advise at the highest level, experience we’re looking for:
   

  

- Technology-driven transformational change experience for the defence/military industry (Försvarsmakten, FMV, FRA, Fortifikationsverket, Swedish Armed Forces) or experience working within a military-industrial organisation;

- An understanding of consulting and strategy principles/frameworks (ideally gained from a consulting firm) would be advantageous;

- MBA qualification desirable;

- An excellent understanding of the trends within the defence industry and the wider Public Sector

- Experience in one or more of:

* CIO Advisory Services
* Value Realisation and Programme assurance
* End-to-end Technology-led Transformation
* Applications modernization
* Cloud transformation
* Sourcing and Contract Optimisation
* Ideally experienced in leading IT strategy engagements, and contributing to sales pursuits and proposal writing


**Why Join Us?**
 This is a unique opportunity to join a world-class consulting team with a proven track record, you’ll work alongside experts who are committed to delivering meaningful change.
   

  

**Our Team Culture**
 At Gartner Consulting Nordics, we believe that great work starts with great people. You’ll be part of a team that is anchored in shared values, deep technology expertise, and a strong sense of purpose. We foster a culture of trust, collaboration, and continuous learning—where every voice is valued and every contribution matters.
   

  

 We also believe in flexibility. Our consultants enjoy the freedom to work from wherever they perform best—whether that’s from home, the office, or on-site with clients. What unites us is not where we work, but how we work: with integrity, curiosity, and a commitment to making a difference.
   

  

**Who are we?**
 At Gartner, Inc. (NYSE:IT), we guide the leaders who shape the world.
   

  

 Our mission relies on expert analysis and bold ideas to deliver actionable, objective business and technology insights, helping enterprise leaders and their teams succeed with their mission-critical priorities.
   

  

 Since our founding in 1979, we’ve grown to 21,000 associates globally who support ~14,000 client enterprises in ~90 countries and territories. We do important, interesting and substantive work that matters. That’s why we hire associates with the intellectual curiosity, energy and drive to want to make a difference. The bar is unapologetically high. So is the impact you can have here.
   

  

**What makes Gartner a great place to work?**
 Our vast, virtually untapped market potential offers limitless opportunities – opportunities that may not even exist right now – for you to grow professionally and flourish personally. How far you go is driven by your passion and performance.
   

  

 We hire remarkable people who collaborate and win as a team. Together, our singular, unifying goal is to deliver results for our clients.
   

  

 Our teams are inclusive and composed of individuals from different geographies, cultures, religions, ethnicities, races, genders, sexual orientations, abilities and generations.
   

  

 We invest in great leaders who bring out the best in you and the company, enabling us to multiply our impact and results. This is why, year after year, we are recognized worldwide as a great place to work.
   

  

**What do we offer?**
 Gartner offers world-class benefits, highly competitive compensation and disproportionate rewards for top performers.
   

  

 In our hybrid work environment, we provide the flexibility and support for you to thrive — working virtually when it's productive to do so and getting together with colleagues in a vibrant community that is purposeful, engaging and inspiring.
   

  

 Ready to grow your career with Gartner? Join us.
   

  

 The policy of Gartner is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, citizenship status, age, national origin, ancestry, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected status and to seek to advance the principles of equal employment opportunity.
   

  

 Gartner is committed to being an Equal Opportunity Employer and offers opportunities to all job seekers, including job seekers with disabilities. title: Kravanalytiker inom Data Warehouse
description:
**Att jobba som kravanalytiker inom Data Warehouse**
 Som kravanalytiker inom Data Warehouse ansvarar du tillsammans med teamet för Skandias informationsförsörjning, informationsstyrning samt data & analytics. Ett område som är helt avgörande för att vi ska kunna bli en ännu mer datadriven och kundfokuserad organisation. Vi är ett agilt utvecklingsteam som idag består av arkitekter, verksamhetsutvecklare, datamodellerare, systemspecialister och kravanalytiker där alla utgår från Lindhagensgatan 86 i Stockholm.
   

  

 Arbetet Är Både Strategiskt Och Operativt, Där Du Är Bryggan Mellan Verksamheten Och IT. I Vardagen Innebär Det Bland Annat Att Du
   

  

* Samlar in, dokumenterar och prioriterar krav från olika intressenter
* Genomför workshops, intervjuer och kravinsamlingssessioner
* Kartlägger och analyserar affärsprocesser, arbetsflöden och informationsstruktur
* Arbetar med datamodellering och identifiering av master- och referensdata
* Arbetar med kravställning kopplat till ETL-processer
* Stöttar utvecklingsteam i tolkning av krav och lösningsdesign


 Vi arbetar hybrid vilket innebär arbete på kontoret varvat med distansarbete. I rollen kommer du att rapportera till gruppchef inom Data & Analytics.
   

  

**För att lyckas i rollen**
 Vi söker en person som är både målmedveten, strukturerad och analytisk. Du kommunicerar tydligt, ibland komplexa sammanhang, lyssnar aktivt och har lätt för att bygga broar mellan olika delar av organisationen. Din samarbetsförmåga är en styrka och du drivs av att hitta lösningar som skapar nytta långsiktigt – både för verksamheten och för slutanvändaren.
   

  

 För att lyckas i rollen har du arbetat som kravanalytiker i flera år gärna från projekt kopplade till data warehouse eller liknande miljöer. Du har arbetat med informationsmodellering och kravställning kopplade till ETL-processer. Du är också van vid att facilitera workshops, samla in krav från olika intressenter och arbeta nära utvecklingsteam. Ett grundkrav i rollen är att du även kan svenska flytande, både i tal och skrift.
   

  

 Ett extra plus i rollen är om du tidigare har arbetat inom bank-/ finansbranschen.
   

  

**Vad händer nu?**
 Vi jobbar med löpande urval så tveka inte att skicka in din ansökan redan idag. I ansökan kommer vi att ställa ett antal urvalsfrågor som ligger till grund för första urvalet. För att göra rekryteringsprocessen så rättvis och objektiv som möjligt kommer det att ingå intervjuer, tester, bakgrundskontroll samt digital referenstagning.
   

  

 Läs mer om vår rekryteringsprocess här.
   

  

 Om du har frågor kring rollen, vänligen kontakta rekryterande chef Hellen Malmberg på hellen.malmberg@skandia.se.
   

  

 Om du har frågor kopplat till rekryteringsprocess, vänligen ta kontakt med Talent Acquistion Partner Susanne Hedberg på susanne.hedberg@skandia.se
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!
   

  

**Mer om Finance**
 Inom Finance arbetar vi
 *framåt tillsammans* 
 för att vara en affärsnära partner och skapa långsiktigt värde för Skandia. Totalt sett består vi av fem olika avdelningar med ungefär 120 medarbetare som alla sitter i Stockholm på Lindhagensgatan 86.
   

  

 Våra avdelningar inom Finance är: Finance Transformation & Infomation , Accounting & Reporting, Aktuarie & Modellering, Inköp & Support samt Group Planning & Control.
   

  

 Tillsammans strävar vi efter att skapa ett effektivt och värdeskapande Finance. Vi arbetar proaktivt för att säkerställa effektiva kärnprocesser genom att följa externa och interna krav. Vårt huvuduppdrag är att stödja koncernledningen med analyser och strategiska beslutsunderlag samt övriga verksamhetsområden inom koncernen. Vi stödjer även våra funktioner med finansiell analys och rapportering. Vårt gemensamma arbete ska hålla hög kvalitet och skapa värde för Skandia. Tillsammans optimerar vi financeområdet genom nya teknologier, arbetssätt och kompetenser. Detta åstadkommer vi genom gott ledarskap, samarbete och engagemang.

title: Head of Legal
description:
**Why Join Us?**
 We’re on a mission to empower people with disabilities to do what they once did or never thought possible. As the world-leader in assistive communication solutions, we empower our customers to express themselves, connect with the world, and live richer lives.
   

  

 At Tobii Dynavox, you can grow your career within a dynamic, global company that has a clear, impactful purpose - with the flexibility to also do what truly matters to you outside of work. What’s more, you’ll be part of a work culture where collaboration is the norm and individuality is welcomed.
   

  

 As a member of our team, you’ll have the power to grow ideas in an unconventional environment. At the same time, you’ll work in a culture of ongoing learning and development, allowing you to constantly expand your area of expertise.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 This is an excellent opportunity for someone who wants to operate in a business-facing role at an international company leading and executing our legal strategy. Besides strategy related work, you will be hands-on with drafting contracts and negotiating a wide range of complex commercial agreements and more.
   

  

 The role sits within the Finance department and is part of the Finance management team. You will have close collaboration with the CFO and work cross‑functionally with product development, sourcing, and other functions. You will also act as a trusted advisor to stakeholders and lead the Legal team, including three direct reports. The team is responsible for legal, IP, trademarks, compliance, privacy, and data protection (GDPR/DPO/HIPAA).
   

  

 The aim with the legal function is to streamline and integrate it closer to the business, while maintaining a smaller team size.
   

  

 Most of your stakeholders are working from our Stockholm (Sweden) and Pittsburgh (US) offices, and our aim is for you to spend the majority of your time working from the Stockholm office, with the option to work remotely.
   

  

**What You’ll Bring**
 We believe you have a good understanding of technology and how product development can have implications from rules and regulations. You have strong acumen for clear risk-versus-reward decisions, for identifying threats and opportunities, and the social capabilities of a strong leader. We think you are great at fostering strong relationships, inside and outside your team, and translating legal matters into simple and clear ways for stakeholders as well as mentoring your team for growth.
   

  

 Since a big part of our operation is situated in the US and Europe, having an understanding of global/US regulations is considered a merit. We are actively working with M&A and we value such experience as a bonus.
   

  

**Minimum Qualifications**
* Law degree from a Swedish or U.S law school or similar.
* At least 3 years of experience in a legal leadership role including team management.
* Experience in developing and executing legal strategy, as well as hands-on experience with complex commercial agreements.


 Please submit your application in English.
   

  

**Apply today!**
 We believe in empowering individuals - including our own employees - to reach their full potential. So, if you want to change lives while growing your own career, we’d love to hear from you.
   

  

**Where we stand:**
 We believe diversity not only enriches our workplace culture, but also gives us a strategic advantage. Working with people from a variety of backgrounds and perspectives helps us all become better communicators, better problem solvers, and better human beings. Our differences make us stronger.
   

  

 Tobii Dynavox values equality of opportunity, human dignity, and racial/ethnic and cultural diversity. Tobii Dynavox does not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, disability, age, veteran status, ancestry, or national or ethnic origin.
   

  

 Equal Opportunity Employer/AA Women/Minorities/Veterans/Disabled

title: GTM Recruiter
description:
**TL;DR** 
 - We’re looking for a GTM recruiter to fuel Lovable’s strategic growth. In this role, you will partner closely with hiring managers across Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Partnerships to design and execute our hiring plan. You’ll be responsible for identifying and attracting world-class top 1% GTM talent through creative sourcing strategies, building strong networks within the GTM community, and ensuring every candidate enjoys a smooth, “lovable” hiring experience.
   

  

**Why Lovable?**
 Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with plain English. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Over 2 million people in 200+ countries already use Lovable to launch businesses, automate work, and bring their ideas to life. And we’re just getting started.
   

  

 We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.
   

  

**What We're Looking For**
* Experience as a GTM Recruiter, preferably within a high-growth SaaS or tech company.
* Strong understanding of GTM roles and the skills required at different levels.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to partner closely with hiring managers and build strong candidate relationships.
* Proficiency in using ATS (Ashby) and recruitment tools to manage pipelines, outreach, and reporting.
* Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and balance multiple searches simultaneously.
* A proactive mindset: you don’t wait for great people to apply, you creatively source the best GTM talent in the world.


**What You’ll Do**
* Partner with hiring managers to scope roles and craft compelling job descriptions that attract world-class GTM talent.
* Source candidates across LinkedIn, networks, events, communities, and referrals.
* Run structured interviews to assess skills, track record, and cultural fit.
* Ensure an excellent candidate experience with timely communication and thoughtful feedback.
* Stay close to industry trends in GTM recruiting, compensation, and market dynamics to sharpen our hiring strategy.
* Contribute to employer branding by representing Lovable within the GTM community.
* Extra plus if you are a passionate user of Lovable.


**About Your Application**
* Please submit your application in English. It’s our company language so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join.
* We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested please apply through our careers portal

title: Sales Development Representative - Nordics

title: Product Manager Nova - Debt Collection - Tieto Banktech (m/f/d)
description:
**Job Description**
**Location: Solna, Sweden**
**Department: Credit & Wealth**
 Tieto Banktech powers some of the Nordics’ most trusted banking systems with secure, reliable, and scalable solutions. Using automation, data, AI, and cloud, supported by hands-on guidance, we help banks modernize their core, strengthen trust, and deliver greater impact across the banking experience.
   

  

 We are now seeking an experienced
 **Product Manager** 
 to play a strategic and influential role in shaping the future of
 **Nova – our market‑leading Debt Collection system** 
 . This role offers the opportunity to drive product vision, market positioning, development, and delivery across the Nordics and Europe. Working closely with other Product Managers and cross‑functional teams, you will ensure Nova remains the most efficient, automated, and competitive solution in the Debt Collection industry.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Product Vision & Strategy:**
 Develop and execute a comprehensive product strategy aligned with business goals and market trends, ensuring it is clearly communicated internally and externally.
   

  

**Market Analysis**
 Conduct deep market and competitive analysis to identify opportunities, threats, and trends across capability, pricing, and technology.
   

  

**Product Development**
 Collaborate with engineering and design teams to translate concepts into delivery. Ensure that the product vision and strategy are executed efficiently and effectively.
   

  

**Product Management**
 Ensure full Product Life Cycle Management, including documentation and governance.
   

  

**Product Marketing**
 Drive product positioning, segmentation, and differentiation. Enable sales and customer success with materials, insights, and market messaging. Contribute to customer meetings across sales, innovation, business development, RFP processes, and escalations.
   

  

**Product Roadmap**
 Define and manage the product roadmap, prioritizing enhancements based on customer needs, feedback, and market dynamics.
   

  

**User‑Centric Approach**
 Advocate for intuitive, secure, and user-friendly product experiences that meet high standards of trust and operational excellence.
   

  

**Collaboration**
 Work closely with other Product Managers, Product Owners, and the entire Debt Collection unit.
   

  

**Cross-Functional Collaboration**
 Partner with marketing, sales, engineering, and customer success teams to ensure successful launches and ongoing customer satisfaction.
   

  

**Compliance & Security**
 Ensure all Nova-related products comply with relevant regulatory and security standards.
   

  

**Required Skills & Competencies**
* Proven experience as a Product Manager within fintech, banking, or related industries
* Extensive knowledge of the Debt Collection industry in the Nordics and/or Europe
* Experience from product modernization journeys
* Strategic thinker with a track record of delivering successful products
* Strong collaboration skills in cross-functional environments
* Experience with strategic pricing, financial performance, cost calculations, and budgeting
* Ability to simplify complex information and communicate clearly to varied audiences
* Good pre‑sales capabilities and excellent communication skills in English
* Knowledge of AI in product development or ways of working is an advantage


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field; MBA or equivalent advanced degree is a plus
* Experience working in product organizations with strong tech–business collaboration
* Familiarity with Nordic languages
* Experience contributing to enterprise-level financial products


**We Offer**
 We offer a unique opportunity to shape the future direction of a leading Debt Collection platform, influence product strategy, and contribute to a modern, high-impact product organization. You will work on meaningful initiatives that directly impact our customers’ efficiency, automation capabilities, and competitive edge — all within an open culture, flexible hybrid work model, and a strong focus on continuous learning and collaboration.
   

  

**Interested?**
 We look forward to receiving your application! Interviews will be held continuously, so we encourage you to apply today. For more information or questions, please contact our Talent Acquisition team at
 **recruitmentinbanking@tieto.com** 
 .
   

  

 We perform background checks on all final candidates.
   

  

 Tieto declines calls from recruitment companies.
   

  

 Additional Information
   

  

 At Tieto, we believe in the power of diversity, equity, and inclusion. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, genders (m/f/d), and walks of life to join our team, as we believe that this fosters an inspiring workplace and fuels innovation. Our commitment to openness, trust, and diversity is at the heart of our mission to create digital futures that benefit businesses, societies, and humanity.

title: Erfaren Enterprise Architect med affärssinne? Vi söker dig!
description:
Vill du vara med och forma framtidens industriella landskap? Vi söker en vass och självgående Enterprise Architecture-rådgivare med ett strategiskt mindset och passion för att skapa verklig affärsnytta i komplexa organisationer. Hos oss får du arbeta med några av Sveriges mest välkända industribolag – i uppdrag där du gör skillnad på riktigt, tillsammans med andra seniora specialister.
   

  

 Your future duties and responsibilities
   

  

 Som Enterprise Architect hos oss får du en nyckelroll i att driva och förankra arkitektur- och transformationsinitiativ. Vanliga uppgifter inkluderar:
   

  

* Ta fram och förankra EA-roadmaps och målbilder
* Rådgivning kring styrning, investeringar och förändringsinitiativ
* Bedömning och design av applikations- och informationslandskap
* Leda EA-forum och stötta beslutsfattare i strategiska teknikval
* Bygga broar mellan IT och verksamhet i transformationsresor


**Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role**
 Vi söker dig som har:

* Konsulterfarenhet och vana att arbeta nära beslutsfattare
* Förmåga att kommunicera komplexa idéer på ett tydligt och affärsnära sätt
* Kommersiellt mindset och förmåga att bygga långsiktiga relationer


 Det är meriterande om du har:

* Kunskap inom ett eller flera av följande områden: Industriell digitalisering / smart industri (Industry 4.0) Produktlivscykelhantering (PLM) & digitala tvillingar Informationsmodellering & datastyrning (data governance) Hållbarhetsrapportering och ESG-drivna IT-initiativ Modernisering av legacy / ERP-landskap i molnmiljöer


 Viktiga kompetenser och egenskaper:
   

  

* Strategisk och analytisk förmåga – du ser helheten och kopplar IT till affärsmål
* Arkitekturkompetens – du behärskar modellering, design och styrning
* Kommunikationsförmåga – du bygger förtroende och leder dialoger med ledning
* Förändringsledning – du har erfarenhet av att driva transformationer

* TOGAF®, ArchiMate®
* Certified Business Architect (CBA®)
* SAFe® Architect / Agilist, ICP-ENT
* AWS / Azure / Google Cloud Architect
* CDMP, COBIT®


 Vad vi erbjuder:

* Flexibla arbetsformer och fokus på balans i livet
* Kontinuerlig kompetensutveckling och kunskapsdelning


 Några av våra förmåner är bland annat
   

  

* Aktieprogram och delägarskap: Som delägare har du möjlighet att påverka och i vårt aktieprogram matchar CGI ditt bidrag, krona för krona upp till 3% av din månadslön.
* Friskvårdsbidrag: Vi erbjuder ett årligt friskvårdsbidrag på 4000 kr.
* Föräldralön: Vi betalar upp till cirka 90% av din lön under den första tiden hemma.
* Trygg anställning: Våra konsulter anställs med kollektivavtal och försäkringar.
* Pensionslösningar: Vi erbjuder attraktiva och förmånliga pensionslösningar.


 Låter det intressant?
   

  

 Vi ser fram emot att höra ifrån dig! Skicka in din ansökan redan idag då urvalet sker löpande. Har du frågor om rollen, kontakta gärna Farzad Ahmed på farzad.ahmed@cgi.com. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!
   

  

**Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.**
 Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…
   

  

 You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.
   

  

 Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.
   

  

 You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.
   

  

 Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

title: Konsulter till myndighetsteam – Affärssystem Raindance
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Vill du arbeta med samhällsnyttiga kunder, ny teknik och en roll där din kompetens gör konkret skillnad? Som konsult hos oss på CGI Raindance får du vara med och forma framtidens ekonomilösningar för svenska myndigheter – i ett växande team där delaktighet, flexibilitet och utvecklingsmöjligheter står i centrum.
   

  

 Är flexibla arbetsförhållanden, eget ansvar och att ha roligt på jobbet viktigt för dig? Då är rollen som konsult med inriktning mot myndigheter hos oss på Raindance perfekt – här får du stort ansvar, möjlighet att bidra med din kunskap från dag ett och samtidigt ha kul på vägen!
   

  

 Raindance är ett av Sveriges mest använda affärssystem inom offentlig sektor. Visste du att åtta av tio personer bor i en region som använder Raindance – och att över 100 kommuner redan är våra kunder? Nu växer vi även snabbt inom myndighetsområdet och bygger upp ett nytt team i Stockholm.
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

 I rollen som konsult med inriktning mot myndigheter får du en nyckelroll i att skapa, anpassa och vidareutveckla våra lösningar i Raindance – i nära samarbete med kollegor och vår utvecklings- och supportorganisation.
   

  

 I rollen ingår bland annat att:
   

  

* Ha eget ansvar i dina uppdrag och möjlighet att påverka ett nytt arbetssätt som omfattar både konsult och support av våra kunder
* Vara med och bygga upp ett helt nytt team med fokus på myndigheter
* Agera rådgivare och stöd i ekonomiprocesser
* Analysera verksamhetens behov och föreslå förbättringar
* Implementera nya kundlösningar i Raindance
* Jobba med support av våra befintliga myndighetskunder
* Arbeta i kundprojekt i nära samverkan med både utveckling och support
* Hålla i utbildningar och stötta användare


 Vi tror på dig och du får ansvar redan från start. Uppdragen för våra myndighetskunder är varierade och sker både självständigt och i team, ofta i projektform.
   

  

 En viktig framgångsfaktor för oss inom Raindance är ett gott samarbetsklimat och en härlig gemenskap!
   

  

 Teamet i Stockholm består idag av 17st härliga konsulter som jobbar med olika uppdrag och projekt tillsammans med våra kunder. Vi har olika erfarenhet, bakgrund och ålder men gemensamt för oss alla är att vi tycker att det är viktigt att vi stöttar varandra och har kul tillsammans!
   

  

 Resor inom Sverige förekommer några gånger per år.
   

  

 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 För att trivas i den här rollen tror vi att du är en nyfiken problemlösare som har en god förståelse för ekonomiprocesser och system – och gärna erfarenhet av arbete inom eller mot myndigheter eller offentlig sektor.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av ekonomiprocesser och affärssystem
* Tidigare roll som t.ex. affärssystemkonsult, controller, systemförvaltare eller ekonom
* Kunskap inom ekonomistyrning, redovisning och/eller beslutsstöd
* Förmåga att arbeta självständigt och driva dina uppdrag framåt
* Flytande svenska i tal och skrift samt goda kunskaper i engelska
* Relevant högskoleutbildning (eller motsvarande yrkeserfarenhet)


 Meriterande men inte ett krav:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av Raindance eller liknande affärssystem (t.ex. Agresso, SAP, Visma)
* Erfarenhet av arbete inom myndigheter eller offentlig sektor
* Tidigare roll som t.ex. affärssystemkonsult, controller, systemansvarig eller ekonom


 Vi värdesätter prestigelöshet, samarbete och viljan att dela med sig av kunskap. Hos oss bidrar alla till att bygga en inkluderande och lärande kultur.
   

  

**To applicants: please note that this position requires Swedish language skills on a professional level, verbally as well as in writing.**
 Om du har några frågor är du välkommen att höra av dig till Karin Mårdsjö i Stockholm karin.mardsjo@cgi.com
   

  

 Vi jobbar löpande med urval och intervjuer och tillsätter tjänsten så snart vi hittar rätt kandidat.
   

  

 Skicka gärna in din ansökan så snabbt som möjligt! Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig!
   

  

**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Säljande managementkonsult
description:
Vill du ta scenen och träna säljare och ledare i nya beteenden? Välkommen till Lime Intenz!
   

  

**Följ med på vår tillväxtresa!**
 På Lime Intenz vill vi se fler företag leverera riktigt bra upplevelser till sina kunder. Vår passion ligger i att skapa framgångsrika säljorganisationer samt att utveckla ledarskap och en proaktiv företagskultur. Hela vår idé går ut på att träna kunder i bästa möjliga beteenden.
   

  

 I dagsläget är Lime Intenz verksamma i Sverige och Norden, men det slutar så klart inte där. Lime Technologies expanderar på den europeiska marknaden och det öppnar upp för spännande möjligheter även för oss på Lime Intenz.
   

  

 Intenz-teamet består idag av sex goa kollegor och därutöver kommer du att få cirka 500 nya kollegor runt om i Europa. Teamet lever efter Pippis motto ”Det har jag aldrig provat förut, så det klarar jag helt säkert”. Idéer, den ständiga viljan att bli bättre och att kasta sig ut i det okända råder det alltså ingen brist på bland dina nya kollegor.
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om oss? Du kan läsa om vad vi sysslar med och lära känna oss bättre här .
   

  

**Vad kommer du att jobba med?**
 Rollen består av en spännande kombination av försäljning och leverans av utbildningar och workshops inom förändringsledning, säljträning, målstyrning och säljledarskap. Från start kommer du följa med seniora kollegor ut, tränas i våra nuvarande paket och modeller och kommer tidigt att få kliva in och hjälpa nya Lime CRM-kunder med deras förändringsresa för att ge dem rätt förutsättningar att komma i gång med sitt nya CRM-system. Här har du möjlighet att jobba med merförsäljning utifrån varje kunds specifika behov för att bli en långsiktig samarbetspartner. Troligen är nästa steg på deras resa att träna sina säljfärdigheter eller att jobba bättre med målstyrning i och utanför sitt nya verktyg. Du kommer även få leverera på insålda projekt där du tränas och coachas inför dina utbildningar och workshops.
   

  

 För att sälja in våra paket och bygga upp din egen kundstock har du den gyllene möjligheten att primärt prospektera på Limes befintliga kunder och genom leads från organisationen, genom tajt samarbete med CRM-säljare och projektledare. Du har också frihet att bearbeta andra bolag som du anser behöver din hjälp. Du ansvarar för hela säljkedjan med dina kunder, men i teamet hittar du stöttning och bollplank av kollegor och chefer som gärna hejar på.
   

  

 Vid leverans kommer du att träffa kunden antingen fysiskt eller digitalt, med allt från 5 till 15 deltagare där du ska utmana deras beteende och säljkultur samt träna dem i nya säljtekniker. Det är utmanande, otroligt kul och ger en kick som är riktigt svår att hitta någon annanstans, om du frågar oss. Målgrupperna för dina workshops är ofta ledare, säljare eller kundservice och uppläggen är allt från korta insatser över 3–6 månader till större strategiska upplägg som återkommer under flera år.
   

  

**Kvalifikationer**
**Vem letar vi efter?**
* Du har 4-8 års erfarenhet av IT- eller komplex B2B-försäljning där du självständigt har drivit hela säljprocessen med framgångsrika resultat
* Du brinner för försäljning och ser fram emot att lära dig att sälja och leverera något som för dig kanske är helt nytt
* Du utstrålar trygghet och pondus och kan ta dig an ett rum med för dig okända personer
* Du tar ansvar för samt strukturerar upp ditt eget arbete
* Du har erfarenhet av, eller intresse för, att arbeta med AI
* Du har en eftergymnasial utbildning
* Du kommunicerar obehindrat på både svenska och engelska i tal och skrift (extra meriterande om du även behärskar något annat nordiskt språk)
* Tidigare erfarenhet av säljledning, coachning och/eller interaktiva grupputbildningar är ett stort plus


**Placering**
 Just nu söker vi en framtida kollega i Stockholm och en i Lund. Du utgår från ditt lokala kontor och arbetar nära våra kunder genom både fysiska möten och digitala leveranser. I rollen ingår resor inom Sverige och vid behov även i Norden, med varierande intensitet.
   

  

**Ingen tid att vänta – sök nu!**
 Vi rekryterar löpande och tillsätter tjänsten så fort vi hittar rätt person.

title: GenAI/Agentic Sr. Customer Solutions Manager , EMEA ISV CSMs
description:
**Description**
 The EMEA ISV Customer Solutions Managers team drives modernization acceleration and cloud-native transformation for AWS's most strategic ISV customers across EMEA. We operate at the intersection of technology depth and business impact, delivering outcomes that reshape how software companies build, monetize, and scale.
   

  

 The ISV AI CSM is the Area leader's Agentic/GenAI acceleration partner for the ISV segment (software and technology companies), working on behalf of the area leader and local SA leader to inspect, unblock, and accelerate Agentic/GenAI business across ISV customers. They operate horizontally across the ISV segment, focusing on the highest-impact Agentic/GenAI opportunities rather than only on a
   

  

 fixed set of CSM-invested accounts. The ISV AI CSM combines deep technical and product understanding of Agentic/GenAI with strong execution and stakeholder management to turn interest and PoCs
   

  

 into production systems and revenue for software and technology companies.
   

  

 Engagement model with Area and local
   

  

**Teams**
* Acts as the Area leader's Agentic/GenAI program owner for the ISV segment: inspects pipeline and live systems, tracks progress on top ISV opportunities, surfaces risks, and recommends corrective actions in regular Area reviews.
* Partners with local SA leaders and CSMs as a force multiplier, joining the most strategic ISV customer engagements (exec briefings, roadmap sessions, design reviews) and then disseminating ISVspecific Agentic/GenAI learnings back into the field.
* Balances depth and scale by going deep on a small number of top ISV opportunities while building mechanisms (guides, templates, training, clinics) that raise the Agentic/GenAI execution bar for all ISV teams in the Area.
* Owns a clear set of Agentic/GenAI acceleration metrics for the ISV segment (for example number of live ISV systems, attach to priority services, velocity from PoC to production, pipeline coverage on target ISVs) and reports them in a simple, leader-friendly way.


 Key job responsibilities
   

  

* Diagnose and prioritize the Area's top Agentic/GenAI ISV opportunities (by revenue, strategic importance, and lighthouse potential) and maintain a clear, inspectable acceleration plan for the ISV segment.
* Proactively identify bottlenecks (technical, organizational, or commercial) on these ISV opportunities and drive concrete actions to unblock them together with Sales, SAs, CSMs, Specialists, ProServe, BD, and service teams.
* Provide hands-on consultative support on Agentic/GenAI architectures, RAG patterns, evaluation, governance, and operating models tailored to software and technology companies, enabling local SAs and CSMs to move ISVs from experiments to production.
* Design and drive "time-to-first-agentic-system" motions for ISVs, ensuring customers have a clear path from the first workshop or PoC
* Codify successful ISV Agentic/GenAI patterns into reusable playbooks (reference architectures, customer journey templates, objection-handling, success metrics) and scale them across the ISV segment in the Area through mechanisms.


**About The Team**
 Diverse Experiences
   

  

 AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

 Why AWS?
   

  

 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
   

  

 Inclusive Team Culture
   

  

 Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.
   

  

 Mentorship & Career Growth
   

  

 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.
   

  

 Work/Life Balance
   

  

 We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* Experience leading complex, large-scale IT/digital/business transformation or migration programs
* Experience with production Agentic/GenAI system delivered with measurable metrics
* Experience managing multiple stakeholders
* Experience operating at both technical depth (architecture conversations) and business level (CXO engagement)
* Evidence of creating reusable mechanisms, playbooks, or templates that scaled beyond a single customer
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including a demonstrated ability to effectively deliver information to the C-suite/VP level
* Bachelor's degree in science, technology, engineering, math, or equivalent experience


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Direct experience implementing AWS/cloud services
* Robust understanding of key technology and market trends
* Deep knowledge of software development/engineering life-cycle from conception to delivery
* PMP and/or SCRUM/Agile, SAFe certified


 Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
   

  

 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 - AWS EMEA SARL (Sweden Branch)

title: Azure Devops Engineer - Microsoft Fabric
description:
Role Summary We are seeking an experienced Azure DevOps & Microsoft Fabric Engineer with strong Power BI platform responsibilities to enable, stabilize, and continuously improve the enterprise analytics ecosystem.
 



 The role focuses on platform engineering, automation, lifecycle management, governance, and release operations for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI, ensuring that business-critical reporting services are reliable, secure, compliant, and aligned with organizational change and licensing policies.
 



 You will act as a technical custodian of the analytics platform, enabling adoption, guiding users, and ensuring that solutions are delivered and operated according to enterprise standards.
 



 Key Responsibilities Azure DevOps & CI/CD (Analytics & BI Delivery)
 



 • Design, build, and maintain Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines for Microsoft Fabric and Power BI assets across Dev/Test/Prod environments
 



 • Ensure that system release deployments for Fabric and Power BI are delivered in compliance with Change Management processes
 



 • Implement versioning, approvals, rollback strategies, and release documentation
 



 • Automate environment configuration, deployment validation, and operational checks Microsoft Fabric Enablement & Platform Improvement
 



 • Enable Microsoft Fabric adoption and continuously improve the technical setup and platform architecture
 



 • Implement and maintain Fabric components (OneLake, Lakehouse, Warehouse, Data Factory, pipelines, workspaces)
 



 • Drive technical platform improvements and automation to enhance stability, scalability, and operational efficiency
 



 • Collaborate with engineering and analytics teams to align Fabric capabilities with business-critical reporting needs Power BI Platform Operations & Stability
 



 • Ensure high availability and stability of Power BI reporting services used for business-critical decision-making
 



 • Support the full lifecycle of Power BI assets (semantic models, reports, apps, refresh processes)
 



 • Monitor, troubleshoot, and resolve platform issues impacting users’ access to data
 



 • Implement operational improvements to ensure customers and stakeholders have reliable access to trusted data Platform Lifecycle Management & User Guidance
 



 • Act as platform owner for Fabric and Power BI from a technical perspective
 



 • Provide guidance and support to system users, BI developers, and data engineers
 



 • Maintain control over system lifecycle activities, including: o Patching and upgrading platform components o Configuration management
 



 o Access management (Entra ID, RBAC, service principals) o Installing and enabling new platform features
 



 • Continuously improve the technical platform in line with evolving business requirements Governance, Compliance & Licensing
 



 • Ensure analytics applications and platforms are installed, configured, and used according to purchased licenses and contractual conditions
 



 • Collaborate with governance, security, and procurement teams to maintain compliance
 



 • Enforce platform governance standards including access controls, auditability, segregation of environments, and documentation
 



 • Support license awareness and optimization across Fabric and Power BI usage Operational Excellence & Monitoring
 



 • Establish monitoring and alerting using Azure Monitor, Log Analytics, and Fabric/Power BI monitoring tools
 



 • Maintain runbooks and operational procedures for incident handling and platform recovery
 



 • Drive continuous improvement in reliability, automation, and platform maturity
 



 Required Skills & Experience Core Requirements
 



 • Strong hands-on experience with Azure DevOps (Pipelines, Repos, YAML, release management)
 



 • Practical experience with Microsoft Fabric (OneLake, Lakehouse/Warehouse, Data Factory)
 



 • Strong experience operating and governing Power BI platforms in enterprise environments
 



 • Experience managing change, release, and deployment processes
 



 • Solid understanding of SQL; familiarity with Spark/PySpark is beneficial
 



 • Strong knowledge of identity and access management (Entra ID / Azure AD, RBAC)

title: Legal Engineering Associate EMEA
description:
**About Us**
 Legora is on a mission: to redefine how legal work gets done. From the very start we have been very clear about the fact that we are not building a solution for lawyers, we are building it with them, because it is the only way to make sure it gets done the right way; working side-by-side every step of the way.
   

  

 Our AI-native workspace empowers legal professionals not just to work faster - but to ask better questions, unlock new insights. Every day, we push the boundaries of legal tech to make complex processes smarter, faster, and more human. From thousands of documents analysed in minutes to intelligent workflows designed in collaboration with leading practices, we’re turning possibility into reality.
   

  

 Today we are trusted by global firms like Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Bird & Bird and Linklaters in over 40 countries, but we have no plans on stopping here. We ship fast, we iterate effectively, and we scale rapidly - not by accident, but by design.
   

  

 When you join Legora, you become part of a team that believes "good enough" isn’t good enough and that the way to win is together, by empowering lawyers to do their best work with technology that truly understands them. If you’re excited by building from first principles, working with exceptional people, and accelerating change in a high-stakes, high-impact domain—then this is the moment and the place.
   

  

**We’re not just shaping the future of legal tech — we’re defining it. Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**The Role**
 This role sits at the intersection of law, technology, and product—supporting SMB clients across EMEA. You’ll help lean legal teams (often without large innovation/ops functions) adopt and scale Legora in a way that delivers real outcomes quickly.
   

  

 You’ll work closely with Legal Engineers, Customer Success, Product, and Go-To-Market to help clients move from first value to repeatable workflows—while feeding what you learn back into how the product evolves.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
* Support SMB client onboarding, pilots, and adoption across EMEA—helping clients get to value quickly and confidently.
* Configure and run standard use cases end-to-end using templates (e.g. contract review, document analysis, drafting workflows), then refine based on user feedback.
* Deliver clear, tailored demos and training to different audiences—from day-to-day users to decision-makers—focusing on why each feature matters, not just how it works.
* Capture user insights and translate them into actionable product feedback, helping shape improvements that make the platform more scalable and intuitive.
* Document best practices and contribute to playbooks so we can deliver consistently excellent outcomes across a growing SMB client base.
* Own discrete workstreams on wider client programmes, managing timelines, dependencies, and stakeholder expectations with increasing autonomy.


**Who You Are**
 You’ll thrive in this role if you’re curious, structured, and comfortable operating in ambiguity—while still delivering high-quality outcomes.
   

  

* Exposure to and interest in law: you’ve completed an LLB (2:1) or equivalent, with some legal work experience (e.g. paralegal, trainee, intern, in-house placement).
* Experience level: 0–2 years.
* Tech-curious and product-minded: you don’t need to code, but you enjoy experimenting with AI tools, learning product features quickly, and iterating on workflows (including basic prompt engineering).
* Strong communicator: you can translate between lawyers and technologists clearly and confidently, adapting your style to the room.
* Analytical and detail-oriented: you can evaluate outputs, spot issues, and propose pragmatic improvements.
* Proactive and reliable: you follow through, manage expectations, and know when to escalate risks or blockers.
* Comfortable with SMB dynamics: you understand that SMB clients value speed-to-impact, clarity, and practical guidance over long change programmes.


**What Success Looks Like (Associate Level)**
* You execute defined client deliverables with growing autonomy, supporting multiple pilots simultaneously.
* You independently configure standard use cases using templates, explain the setup to users, and capture feedback in crisp notes.
* You deliver strong demos (especially to existing clients on new features), and follow up with clear summaries and next steps.
* You manage specific workstreams on broader programmes—owners, dependencies, deadlines—adjusting quickly when constraints change.
* You build trust with stakeholders through responsiveness, clarity, and reliable delivery.
* You can speak credibly about how AI is impacting legal work, and connect those implications to Legora’s capabilities.


**A few more things**
 Legora is fast-growing, and this is a role with real autonomy and responsibility. If you’re excited by building from first principles, learning quickly, and helping SMB legal teams transform how they work—this is the moment.
   

  

**Ready to join us in building the intelligent future of law?**
**Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer**
 At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: Senior Integration Lead Developer
description:
Beskrivning av tjänsten
   

  

 Vill du vara med i Sveriges vassaste integrations-team? Då är du rätt person för oss! Nu söker vi Dig som vill jobba som Integration Lead Developer på CGI.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder dig som har ett brinnande intresse för integrationsutveckling på Microsoft-plattformen att börja i ett team som arbetar tätt tillsammans. Normalt sitter vi ihop och arbetar från vårt kontor i Stadshagen. Vi arbetar med spännande kunder i alla branscher vilket gör att du ständigt får nya utmaningar och möjlighet att vidareutvecklas.
   

  

 På CGI strävar vi efter att skapa framgångsrika team som har roligt på jobbet och hjälper varandra att utvecklas. CGI är Sveriges ledande IT-företag och ett av världens största. Detta ger fantastiska karriärmöjligheter och möjlighet till spännande uppdrag. Vårt framgångsrika integrationsteam är som ett eget litet företag inom ramarna för stora CGI vilket ger en stark teamkänsla och samhörighet.
   

  

 CGI är Microsoft Gold Partner inom Application Integration. Vårt integrationsteam i Stockholm går för högvarv och behöver förstärkning. Här får Du möjlighet att jobba med några av de bästa integrationsutvecklarna i branschen!
   

  

 Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter
   

  

* Utveckla robusta, säkra och återanvändbara integrationslösningar med hjälp av Azure Integration Services: Logic Apps, Azure Service Bus, API Management, Event Grid och Azure Functions
* Leda tekniska designdiskussioner, definiera integrationsmönster och bidra till lösningsarkitekturen
* Fungera som teknisk ledare för integrationsprojekt och säkerställa överensstämmelse med affärs- och IT-strategier
* Samarbeta nära med lösningsarkitekter, företagsarkitekter och affärsintressenter
* Mentorera och stödja junior-/mellannivåutvecklare och främja bästa praxis och kodkvalitet
* Driva implementeringen av DevOps och automatiseringsmetoder för integrationspipelines (CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code)
* Övervaka integrationsprestanda och proaktivt identifiera förbättringar
* Äga hela livscykeln för integrationer – från kravinsamling till produktionssupport


 Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har:
   

  

* 5 till 8+ års erfarenhet i integrationsutveckling, med stark erfarenhet av att designa och implementera lösningar på Azure iPaas
* Djupgående kunskaper om Azure Logic Apps, Service Bus, API Management, Azure Functions, Event Grid och hybridanslutningsalternativ
* Bevisad meritlista inom design av API-baserade arkitekturer, eventdrivna flöden och molnbaserade integrationer
* Stark kunskap om autentiseringsmekanismer (OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, Azure AD) och bästa praxis för säkerhet i integrationer
* Kunskaper inom CI/CD-pipelines (helst med Azure DevOps)
* Erfarenhet av övervaknings- och observationsverktyg (t.ex. App Insights, Log Analytics)
* Stark förståelse för integrationsmönster, mikrotjänster och tjänsteorienterad arkitektur (SOA)
* Utmärkta kommunikations-, ledarskaps- och intressenthanteringsfärdigheter
* Kunskap om docker och containers
* Obehindrad svenska och engelska i tal och skrift


 Meriterande om du också har:
   

  

* Microsoft-certifieringar (t.ex. Azure Solutions Architect, Azure Developer Associate)
* Erfarenhet av att integrera med SAP, Dynamics 365, Salesforce eller andra on-premise system
* Kännedom om BizTalk-migreringar till Azure eller strategier för modernisering av integrationer
* Exponering för dataströmningsplattformar (t.ex. Kafka, Event Hubs)
* Bakgrund inom agila/Scrum-metoder


**Vad du kan förvänta dig av oss:**
 Integrationsteamet på CGI är ett gäng som trivs ihop, delar kunskap och stöttar varandra. Här är det lätt att vara sig själv, och det finns alltid någon att bolla idéer med. Ibland gör vi något kul tillsammans efter jobbet, som en spelkväll – bara om man vill, såklart!
   

  

**Låter det intressant?**
 Vi ser fram emot att höra ifrån dig! Skicka in din ansökan redan i dag då urvalet sker löpande. Har du frågor om rollen, kontakta gärna rekryterande konsultchef Ramya Parthasarathy, ramya.parthasarathy@cgi.com eller konsultchef Henrik Sjöstedt, henrik,sjostedt@cgi.com.
   

  

**Vad CGI erbjuder dig**
 CGIs kombination av global och lokal närvaro, med kontor på 30 orter i Sverige, ger nöjda kunder men innebär även att du som anställd alltid har ett kontor nära dig. Detta i kombination med flexibla arbetstider ger dig möjlighet till balans mellan privatliv och arbete, oavsett var i livet just du befinner dig.
   

  

 På CGI finns även stora möjligheter till interna karriärvägar där du kan välja att bli specialist inom ditt område, bredda dig inom nya områden eller ta en ledande befattning. Via våra interna nätverk, projekt och åtaganden i globala och nationella industrier, erbjuder vi dig kompetensutveckling och engagerade ledare som stöttar just dig i din utveckling.
   

  

 Mer information om våra förmåner:
   

  

* Aktieprogram och delägarskap: Som delägare har du möjlighet att påverka och i vårt aktieprogram matchar CGI ditt bidrag, krona för krona upp till 3% av din månadslön.
* Friskvårdsbidrag: Vi erbjuder ett årligt friskvårdsbidrag på 4000 kr.
* Föräldralön: Vi betalar upp till cirka 90% av din lön under den första tiden hemma.
* Trygg anställning: Våra konsulter anställs med kollektivavtal och försäkringar.
* Pensionslösningar: Vi erbjuder attraktiva och förmånliga pensionslösningar.
* Personalbil: Personalbilen används som en privatbil men leasas av CGI.


**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**
 Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.
   

  

 Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.
   

  

 Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.
   

  

 Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
   

  

 Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Machine Learning /AI Engineer (Senior to Principal Level)
description:

Join us and collaborate with top minds, build groundbreaking solutions, and make a real difference for our clients. Enjoy extensive professional development, global reach, and a supportive, Google-inspired culture. If you're passionate about the cloud, this is where you want to be.


With over 14 years of experience in Google Cloud under our belt, offices in 18 countries and more than 600 experts, we have deployed practical innovation alongside Google. We focus on infrastructure modernisation, data management, smart analytics, application modernisation, and AI & machine learning.


Curious about our projects? We got you: Check out our success stories

* Collaborate with data scientists, data engineers, and Google experts to overcome complex challenges and deliver impactful outcomes.

* Stay at the forefront of Google Cloud AI/ML technologies, continuously expanding your expertise and exploring new possibilities.
* Confidently present solutions to clients, bridging the gap between complex algorithms and real-world business value, while actively cultivating and expanding those customer relationships.

Want to know more? Check out our people stories on Youtube


  
We're seeking a passionate and experienced ML Engineer with a proven track record in designing, developing, and deploying machine learning models. You'll be a cloud enthusiast with a deep understanding of AI/ML principles and hands-on experience with cloud technologies. As a coding expert, you'll possess strong Python skills and a solid foundation in machine learning frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain, LangGraph).


Excellent communication is essential – you'll build strong relationships with clients, translate their needs into innovative AI solutions, and present complex topics with clarity and confidence. You're a true collaborator, thriving in team environments where knowledge sharing and mutual support are paramount.
  

Fluency in English and one of the Scandinavian languages (Swedish, Norwegian, or Danish) is required


  
- **Why Choose Devoteam G Cloud?**


	* Own the AI revolution: Dive into the exciting world of Generative AI and ML, playing a pivotal role in shaping projects around Google's groundbreaking Gemini foundation model.
	* Innovative Impact: Work on the forefront of cloud technology at a globally recognized leader in Google Cloud solutions.
	* Direct Google Connection: Collaborate closely with Google teams, shaping the future of cloud and AI.
	* Unleash Your Expertise: Thrive in a fast-paced environment where sharing knowledge and success is paramount.
	* End-to-End Impact: Drive complete business transformations, from strategy to implementation and beyond.
	* Accelerate Your Career: Grow within a Google-inspired culture, gaining specialisations and certifications.We do plenty of activities, like meet-ups, hackathons, and partnerships. Don’t lose any of them: follow us on LinkedIn


**Benefits**


	* Encouragement and support for training and certification, in the form of coaching from other team members, cost coverage, and financial incentives.
	* Challenging and rewarding assignments, many of which are end-to-end.
	* A hybrid work model as standard, with flexible working hours and location to support your work-life balance.
	* An international, diverse, and inclusive team.
	* A human-centric and value-based company culture.
	* Wellness grants for sports activities (5,000 SEK/year).
	* A modern office in central Stockholm.
	* Full provision of work tools.
	* A variety of social events.Want to know more about life at Devoteam? Check our Instagram page


Ready to join an elite team? Apply for our Machine Learning & AI Engineer position and become part of something exceptional!


Is this not the right position for you? Don’t worry, we have plenty of opportunities! Check out our Career page

title: Data Center Engineering Cluster Manager, Data Center InfraOps
description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. We're the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on.  

You'll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, and operations managers. You'll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers.  

The Infrastructure Operations organization is seeking an exceptional leader to serve as a Data Center Engineering Operations (DCEO) Cluster Manager in Sweden. You will lead a large organization managing multiple data centers in the cluster, overseeing managers responsible for critical facility infrastructure.  

The pace, scale, and growth of Amazon's data center platform will challenge you. We seek experienced senior leaders who are highly strategic in managing their business unit, yet lead by example and roll up their sleeves when required.  

The ideal candidate will have strong knowledge of data center and mission-critical MEP infrastructure. You will be responsible for maintaining 100% service uptime in our cluster, maintaining a high-level view while diving deep into details as needed. You will interact daily with global teams, representing Sweden Data Center Engineering Operations.  

If you are passionate about customer experience, think and act globally, and want to contribute to the operational excellence of AWS data centers, this is the challenge you've been looking for.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

Team Leadership & Development: Hire, manage, and develop the operations management team including Facility Managers, Chief Engineers, and Facility Technicians. Mentor and develop employees at all levels, including managers.  

  

Operational Excellence: Ensure operation and maintenance of mechanical, electrical, and controls systems for AWS data centers, including preventive maintenance, corrective maintenance, and change management. Maintain 100% service uptime across your cluster.  

  

Performance Management: Establish performance benchmarks, conduct analyses, and prepare reports on all aspects of critical facility operations and maintenance. Manage all facility-related repairs in a timely manner.  

  

Organizational Structure: Build and maintain a sustainable organizational structure. Develop hiring strategy and participate in recruiting events.  

Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work with business development, engineering, and construction teams to forecast staffing needs and maintain appropriate resource levels.  

  

Vendor Management: Manage colocation data center service providers to meet or exceed contracted performance SLAs. Oversee on-site management of shift technicians, senior shift technicians, sub-contractors, and vendors, ensuring all work is performed in accordance with established practices and procedures.  

  

Incident Management: Lead safety, security, and availability incident response, incident management, incident resolution, and root cause analysis.  

  

Financial Management: Manage cost including OPEX and CAPEX associated with all data centers under your responsibility.  

  

Reporting & Communication: Conduct daily/weekly/monthly Data Center Engineering Operations meetings and reporting. Run weekly availability meetings and report outcomes.  

  

Global Integration: Liaise with global teams on initiatives, processes, and procedures; implement those initiatives locally in Sweden.  

  

Continuous Improvement: Drive continuous improvement of operational processes, procedures, methods, and tools. Lead and manage energy efficiency initiatives in your cluster.  

  

Capacity Planning: Support capacity planning and management activities.  

  

About the team  

You'll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You'll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you'll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* Experience hiring, developing, and managing high-performing technical teams.
* 4+ years of experience operating as a manager of managers AND/OR 7+ years of relevant experience managing mission-critical facilities.
* In-depth knowledge of data center facilities such as generators, chillers, cooling towers, air handling units, UPS, electrical sub-distribution systems, fire detection and suppression systems, and cable reticulation systems.
* Fluency in English to communicate effectively with global teams.
* Driving license valid in EU.

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* Bachelor’s or master’s degree in engineering or related fields.
* Experience managing or implementing system health, performance monitoring, capacity planning, or process improvement and automation.
* Experience supporting procedures for production or mission-critical environments including ticketing, monitoring/metrics, and troubleshooting technical issues.
* Fluency in Swedish.

  

Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.  

  

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
